mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.9% in May 2010 reflects post-crisis labor slack
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 366404M in April 2010 shows post-crisis demand recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no correlation with month-over-month RSAFS growth (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero combined with sustained low inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2.5% annually) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime that suppresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.2% and CPIAUCSL at 217.729 in May 2010
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 60% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profitability and equity returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.27 in April 2010 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: post-recession supply-side recovery with capacity expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO growth > 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment signals accelerating labor market tightening and wage pressure, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130161K and UNRATE at 9.9% in April 2010
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase with simultaneous employment gains and unemployment decline
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of instances when both conditions are jointly satisfied.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals weakening construction demand and reduced future consumer wealth effects from real estate appreciation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in slower economic growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 626K in March 2010 reflects post-crisis housing weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines > 5% are positive in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration signals strengthening aggregate demand and economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings expansion and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13254.7B in Q1 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with above-trend GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL growth > 2.5% annually) combined with positive federal funds rate signals monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.729 and FEDFUNDS at 0.2% in May 2010
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of instances when both conditions are jointly met.
